user_text: create a beignner level lesson on what is machine learning. the lesson should be around 20 min long.generate me a pptx file for slides, and a python file with explainations and example codes as well.

messages.content:
You are an expert autonomous Task Orchestrator.
Your goal is to analyze user requests and educational materials, then create a structured execution plan.

Available Agents:
- 'coder': Generates runnable Python code. Best for demonstrations, data analysis, and coding tutorials.
- 'video': Creates video content. Best for visual explanations.
- 'presentation': Generates PowerPoint slides (.pptx). Best for structured lesson plans, summaries, and educational materials.
- 'quizzer': Creates quizzes. Best for assessments and review.

Instructions:
1. Analyze the user's input and provided files.
2. Break down the goal into logical subtasks.
3. Assign each subtask to the most appropriate agent.
4. Provide specific, actionable instructions for each agent.
5. For every subtask, include a non-empty 'acceptance_criteria' list.
   - Each criterion must include: criterion_id, criterion_type, target, operator, expected, severity.
   - Use criterion_type values: mcp_tool | output_shape | file_exists | semantic.
   - Use operator values: equals | contains | exists | gte | lte.
   - Use target paths that start with 'result.' or 'tools.'.
   - For mcp_tool criteria, include tool: { name, args }.
   - Use placeholders like $artifacts[0] or $output.validation.path inside tool args.
6. Use the provided tool 'create_task_plan' to submit your plan.

Response Format Example:
{
  "user_intent": "Create a lesson on Python lists with a video intro and a quiz.",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "task_id": "step1_code",
      "agent": "coder",
      "instruction": "Generate a Python script demonstrating list operations.",
      "inputs": {},
      "dependencies": [],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        {
          "criterion_id": "code_executes",
          "criterion_type": "mcp_tool",
          "tool": {"name": "python_check", "args": {"filepath": "$artifacts[0]"}},
          "target": "tools.python_check.success",
          "operator": "equals",
          "expected": true,
          "severity": "required",
          "evidence": "tools.python_check.run.stderr"
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "task_id": "step2_video",
      "agent": "video",
      "instruction": "Create a video explaining the code.",
      "inputs": {},
      "dependencies": ["step1_code"],
      "acceptance_criteria": [
        {
          "criterion_id": "video_semantic_ok",
          "criterion_type": "semantic",
          "target": "result.output",
          "operator": "exists",
          "expected": true,
          "severity": "required",
          "evidence": "result.output"
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

content.text: create a beignner level lesson on what is machine learning. the lesson should be around 20 min long.generate me a pptx file for slides, and a python file with explainations and example codes as well.

function.name: create_task_plan
function.description: Create a structured execution plan for multiple agents.

type.description: Criterion type: mcp_tool | output_shape | file_exists | semantic
type.title: Type

target.description: Evaluation target path (e.g., result.output.text or tools.python_check.success)
target.title: Target

operator.description: Operator: equals | contains | exists | gte | lte
operator.title: Operator

tool.description: Tool call definition for mcp_tool criteria
tool.title: Tool

evidence.description: Evidence path to include in judger output
evidence.title: Evidence

agent.description: The agent responsible (coder, video, text, quizzer)
agent.title: Agent

dependencies.description: IDs of tasks that must complete first
dependencies.title: Dependencies

acceptance_criteria.description: Structured criteria the judger will use
acceptance_criteria.title: Acceptance Criteria

user_intent.description: Summary of what the user wants to achieve
user_intent.title: User Intent

tool_calls.name: create_task_plan
tool_calls.arguments: {"user_intent":"Create a ~20-minute beginner lesson on Machine Learning, delivered as a PPTX slide deck plus a Python file containing explanations and example code.","subtasks":[{"id":"slides_pptx","agent":"presentation","title":"Create a beginner-friendly slide deck (~20 minutes, ~12–16 slides) explaining what Machine Learning is. Include: definition, why it matters, key terms (features/labels/model/training/inference), types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement) with simple examples, ML workflow, train/test split, overfitting intuition, evaluation metrics (accuracy vs. MAE), and a short recap + Q&A slide. Provide speaker notes with estimated timing per slide. Output as a .pptx file.","dependencies":[],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"pptx_file_exists","type":"file_exists","target":"result.artifacts[0].path","operator":"contains","expected":".pptx","severity":"required","evidence":"result.artifacts[0].path"},{"id":"pptx_has_min_slides","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"12","severity":"optional","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"pptx_contains_key_topics","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"supervised","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"pptx_contains_workflow","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"train/test","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"}]},{"id":"python_lesson_file","agent":"coder","title":"Generate a single Python file that reads like a mini-lesson with rich comments/docstrings (beginner level). Include runnable examples using scikit-learn: (1) simple classification (e.g., Iris or breast cancer) with train/test split, standardization if needed, LogisticRegression, accuracy; (2) simple regression (e.g., California housing or synthetic) with LinearRegression, MAE; (3) brief unsupervised demo with KMeans on Iris features. Include explanations of features/labels, fit/predict, overfitting concept, and how to interpret outputs. Ensure code is runnable as-is with a main() guard and prints results. Save as .py.","dependencies":[],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"py_file_exists","type":"file_exists","target":"result.artifacts[0].path","operator":"contains","expected":".py","severity":"required","evidence":"result.artifacts[0].path"},{"id":"py_contains_main_guard","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"if __name__ == \"__main__\"","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"py_mentions_fit_predict","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"fit","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"py_includes_examples","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"LogisticRegression","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"},{"id":"py_includes_kmeans","type":"semantic","target":"result.output","operator":"contains","expected":"KMeans","severity":"required","evidence":"result.output"}]}]}

parsed_plan.user_intent: Create a ~20-minute beginner lesson on Machine Learning, delivered as a PPTX slide deck plus a Python file containing explanations and example code.

subtasks.instruction: Create a beginner-friendly slide deck (~20 minutes, ~12–16 slides) explaining what Machine Learning is. Include: definition, why it matters, key terms (features/labels/model/training/inference), types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement) with simple examples, ML workflow, train/test split, overfitting intuition, evaluation metrics (accuracy vs. MAE), and a short recap + Q&A slide. Provide speaker notes with estimated timing per slide. Output as a .pptx file.

Generate a single Python file that reads like a mini-lesson with rich comments/docstrings (beginner level). Include runnable examples using scikit-learn: (1) simple classification (e.g., Iris or breast cancer) with train/test split, standardization if needed, LogisticRegression, accuracy; (2) simple regression (e.g., California housing or synthetic) with LinearRegression, MAE; (3) brief unsupervised demo with KMeans on Iris features. Include explanations of features/labels, fit/predict, overfitting concept, and how to interpret outputs. Ensure code is runnable as-is with a main() guard and prints results. Save as .py.

final_plan.user_intent: Create a ~20-minute beginner lesson on Machine Learning, delivered as a PPTX slide deck plus a Python file containing explanations and example code.

subtasks.instruction: Create a beginner-friendly slide deck (~20 minutes, ~12–16 slides) explaining what Machine Learning is. Include: definition, why it matters, key terms (features/labels/model/training/inference), types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement) with simple examples, ML workflow, train/test split, overfitting intuition, evaluation metrics (accuracy vs. MAE), and a short recap + Q&A slide. Provide speaker notes with estimated timing per slide. Output as a .pptx file.

Generate a single Python file that reads like a mini-lesson with rich comments/docstrings (beginner level). Include runnable examples using scikit-learn: (1) simple classification (e.g., Iris or breast cancer) with train/test split, standardization if needed, LogisticRegression, accuracy; (2) simple regression (e.g., California housing or synthetic) with LinearRegression, MAE; (3) brief unsupervised demo with KMeans on Iris features. Include explanations of features/labels, fit/predict, overfitting concept, and how to interpret outputs. Ensure code is runnable as-is with a main() guard and prints results. Save as .py.

python_lesson_file.instruction: Deterministic failure: no .py file was produced (py_file_exists=false) and result.output is empty, so semantic criteria cannot be satisfied. Fix: create and return a single runnable .py file (or include its full contents in result.output) that includes a main guard (if __name__ == "__main__":), uses scikit-learn examples for (1) classification with train_test_split, (optionally StandardScaler), LogisticRegression, accuracy; (2) regression with LinearRegression and MAE; (3) unsupervised KMeans on Iris features. Add beginner-friendly comments/docstrings explaining features/labels, fit/predict workflow, overfitting, and print results when run.

slides_pptx.instruction: No .pptx output file was provided (result.output is null and pptx_file_exists=false), so the submission is unusable. Generate and attach the PowerPoint (.pptx) slide deck with ~12–16 slides and speaker notes including estimated timing per slide. Ensure the deck explicitly includes the required topics/terms: definition of ML, why it matters, key terms (features, labels, model, training, inference), types (supervised/unsupervised/reinforcement) with simple examples, ML workflow, explicit train/test split, overfitting intuition, evaluation metrics (accuracy vs MAE), and a recap + Q&A slide.
